2605.00425.title: AEM: Adaptive Entropy Modulation for Multi-Turn Agentic Reinforcement Learning
2605.00425.paper_summary: Reinforcement learning (RL) has significantly advanced the ability of large language model (LLM) agents to interact with environments and solve multi-turn tasks. Yet effective training remains challenging, as sparse, outcome-only rewards make it difficult to assign credit to individual steps in an agent's action trajectory. A common remedy is to introduce dense intermediate supervision, such as process reward models or auxiliary self-supervised signals, but this increases supervision and tuning complexity and often generalizes poorly across tasks and domains. This paper presents AEM, a supervision-free credit assignment method that adaptively modulates entropy dynamics during RL training to achieve a more effective exploration-exploitation trade-off. Theoretically, we elevate entropy analysis from the token level to the response level to reduce token sampling variance and show that entropy drift under natural gradients is intrinsically governed by the product of the advantage and the relative response surprisal. Specifically, we derive a practical proxy to reshape training dynamics, enabling a natural transition from exploration to exploitation. Extensive experiments across various benchmarks and models ranging from 1.5B to 32B parameters demonstrate the effectiveness of AEM, including a notable 1.4 percent gain when integrated into a state-of-the-art baseline on the highly challenging SWE-bench-Verified benchmark.
2605.00425.paper_summary_zh: 強化學習 (RL) 顯著提升了大型語言模型 (LLM) 代理與環境互動及解決多回合任務的能力。<lb/>然而，有效的訓練仍然具有挑戰性，因為稀疏的僅結果獎勵使得難以將功勞分配給代理行動軌跡中的個別步驟。<lb/>一個常見的解決方案是引入密集的中介監督，例如過程獎勵模型或輔助自我監督信號，但這增加了監督和調整的複雜性，並且通常在任務和領域之間的泛化效果不佳。<lb/>本文提出了 AEM，一種無監督的功勞分配方法，通過自適應調節 RL 訓練期間的熵動態，以實現更有效的探索-利用權衡。<lb/>理論上，我們將熵分析從標記層面提升到回應層面，以減少標記抽樣方差，並顯示自然梯度下的熵漂移本質上由優勢與相對回應驚訝度的乘積所支配。<lb/>具體而言，我們推導出一個實用的代理來重塑訓練動態，使探索到利用的過渡變得自然。<lb/>在各種基準和模型（參數範圍從 1.5B 到 32B）上進行的大量實驗證明了 AEM 的有效性，包括在極具挑戰性的 SWE-bench-Verified 基準上集成到最先進基線時顯著提升 1.4% 的效果。
2605.00425.author: Haotian Zhao et.al.
2605.00425.authors: Haotian Zhao, Yuxin Zhang, Songlin Zhou, Stephen S. -T. Yau, Wenyu Zhang, Lun Tian, Tianshu Zhu, Yifeng Huang, Yucheng Zeng, Jingnan Gu, Daxiang Dong, Jianmin Wu
